description: Tool-rule fixtures for the validation- and version-shaped OG-T rules. OG-T-01 (validate before acting; fail the binding rather than act on a violating graph) has representative cases here; its full obligation is the entire OG-V negative set in OG-VR.json, of which these cases are a sample. OG-T-02 (version refusal on this format's own openbindings.operation-graph field, mirroring core OBI-T-04) is expressed with the same version-gate annotations the core corpus uses, applied to the tool's supported operation-graph format range.
notes: valid:false under OG-T-01 means a conformant tool refuses to act on the graph; valid:false under OG-T-02 means a conformant tool refuses the graph's declared version, reported distinctly from graph non-conformance. OG-T-02 tests assume a tool supporting the 0.2.x line, bounded below 999.x for major refusal and 0.999.x for pre-1.0 minor refusal; downward refusal is gated with requiresMinSupported and post-1.0 forward-compat with requiresMaxTested, mirroring the core corpus's OBI-T-04 fixture. Both blocks declare schemaEnforced: false — the negative graphs here are schema-valid (a cross-reference violation, a refused but syntactically valid version); the schema-rejection assertion belongs to OG-VR.json's schema-enforced rule blocks.

rules.title: A tool acting on an operation-graph binding MUST validate the target graph against OG-V-01..18 and MUST fail the binding rather than act on a violating graph.

tests.description: a graph satisfying the validation rules may be acted on
an edge referencing an undefined node key (OG-V-07, beyond schema) must fail the binding before any action
an orphan node unreachable from input (OG-V-06, beyond schema) must fail the binding before any action

rules.title: Tools MUST refuse graphs declaring an openbindings.operation-graph version outside their supported range (pre-1.0: minor-granular, both directions), MUST NOT accept undeclared prereleases, and compare by SemVer 2.0.0 precedence with build metadata ignored; a higher patch within a supported major.minor never refuses.

tests.description: graph declares the current format version (tool supporting the 0.2.x line accepts)
build metadata is ignored for comparison; 0.2.0+build.1 is treated as 0.2.0 and accepted
a higher patch within a supported major.minor is non-breaking and is never a refusal trigger
a prerelease is not accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (default rejection)
graph declares a major version higher than any plausible tool supports
pre-1.0 graph declares a higher minor than any plausible tool supports (refusal extends to minors while the format is pre-1.0)
post-1.0 forward-compat: a tool supporting major 1 accepts a same-major-higher-minor graph
downward refusal: a graph declaring a minor below the tool's minimum supported version is refused rather than interpreted under the wrong rules
